name: Rock Beat 101
tip: Keep the hi-hat steady on every eighth note. Snare locks in on beats 2 and 4 — that's what makes it groove.

name: Disco Pulse
tip: Hi-hat hits on the off-beats (the "ands"). That upbeat pulse is what drives disco and funk.

name: Half-Time Slap
tip: The snare only hits once per bar, giving a big heavy feel. Kick fills the space — listen for where beat 1 lands.

name: Tom Fill
tip: First half is straight time, second half explodes into toms. Keep the kick locked in when the toms come in.

name: Crash & Ride
tip: Crash on beat 1 signals the top of the form. Ride carries the pulse. Listen to how the crash and ride work together.

name: Shuffle
tip: The shuffle uses a "long-short" pattern on the hi-hat. Feel the triplet swing — it's what separates blues from rock.

name: Punk Bash
tip: Fast and aggressive. Hi-hat never lets up. The snare stutters between backbeats — stay relaxed, keep your wrist loose.

name: Funk Pocket
tip: Ghost notes and syncopated kick define the pocket. Every hit matters — listen for the spaces between the notes.
